definition: Covenant-lite structures (>90% of institutional loans) with 25-40% EBITDA addbacks impair early warning signals of credit deterioration. True leverage is 5.5-7.5x vs reported 4.0-4.5x. Absence of maintenance covenants eliminates the gradual deterioration pathway, potentially producing bimodal default distributions (healthy-appearing then suddenly distressed).
evidence: No organic full credit cycle without policy rescue (N=0)
Maturity wall of $900B-$1.5T for 2025-2028 cited without comparison to previous successfully refinanced walls
open_questions: Will the bimodal default distribution thesis be validated in the next organic credit cycle?
Can the $900B-$1.5T maturity wall be successfully refinanced like previous walls?
Do rating agencies accept sponsor-provided EBITDA figures, and if so, is the entire credit rating architecture miscalibrated?
What is the base rate for successful maturity wall resolution?
